Ad Text:
At Altus, we’re on a mission to eliminate administrative work through intelligent automation – freeing people to focus on what truly matters. Our Microsoft 365-based Project Portfolio Management platform is growing rapidly, and as we expand our global support model, we’re looking for a Technical Support Engineer in Australia to work alongside our European support function and ensure our partners and customers succeed with Altus.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing Australian technology company with a global footprint. You’ll play a critical role in supporting our international ecosystem – providing expert technical guidance across the Microsoft 365 and Power Platform environment that powers Altus.

If you enjoy solving complex technical problems, helping customers succeed, and working with modern Microsoft technologies, this role offers the chance to make a meaningful impact.

 

Why join Altus?

Be part of a fast-growing global platform built on Microsoft 365 and Power Platform
Work closely with our European support team to deliver seamless global coverage
Leverage flexible working hours (11am–7pm) to support international collaboration
Work with cutting-edge technologies including Power Platform, Dataverse and Microsoft Copilot
Join a collaborative global team spanning Australia and Europe
Help organisations reduce complexity and reclaim time through smarter technology



The Role

As a Technical Support Engineer, you’ll deliver high-quality, efficient and customer-centric technical support to Altus partners and customers globally, working closely with our European support team to provide extended coverage across time zones.

This role follows a structured 11am–7pm schedule, enabling critical overlap with our European team and supporting a seamless global support model.

You’ll act as a second and third level escalation point across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem, Microsoft Power Platform, and the Altus product suite, ensuring issues are resolved quickly and effectively.

Designed to complement our European support function, this role is key to delivering a “follow-the-sun” support model. You’ll collaborate closely with both the European support engineer and the Altus engineering team in Australia to resolve complex issues and continuously improve the platform experience.

This position is central to the success of the Altus global ecosystem – enabling partner success, protecting customer satisfaction, and supporting our continued international growth.

 

What you’ll be doing

Provide advanced technical support across Altus modules and Microsoft 365 components
Troubleshoot complex issues within Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI and Dataverse environments
Identify whether issues relate to product defects, configuration or partner implementations
Maintain accurate support records and manage escalations to Product, Engineering and Leadership teams
Support major incidents and outage management when required
Work closely with the European support engineer to ensure smooth handovers and continuity of support
Act as a technical escalation point for Altus channel partners, supporting troubleshooting and best practices
Assist with partner onboarding and technical knowledge transfer
Support partner-led customer enhancements to ensure platform compatibility
Assist with release validation and environment management, working closely with the Product team
Create and maintain knowledge base articles, troubleshooting guides and documentation
Identify opportunities to improve processes through automation and AI-driven support tools
Communicate clearly and professionally with partners and customers
Participate in technical meetings when required and support solution discussions



About you

You’re a technically curious problem-solver who enjoys helping customers succeed with technology. You’re comfortable working independently, collaborating across time zones, and tackling complex technical challenges. The successful candidate must be comfortable with working a 11am-7pm shift.

You’ll likely bring:

5+ years experience in a technical support or development role
Strong knowledge of the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps (specifically model-driven apps), Power Automate, Power BI and Dataverse
Experience with Support Management Systems
Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
A calm, customer-focused approach when managing technical issues
Passion for Microsoft cloud technologies
Comfortable working a set schedule of 11am–7pm to support global operations

Bonus points if you have experience with:

SharePoint or Microsoft 365 administration
Microsoft Project, Planner or DevOps
Building Power Platform solutions
SQL queries or Power BI reporting

Microsoft certifications and relevant tertiary qualifications are highly valued.

​​​​ Why this role is special

This role sits at the centre of Altus’s global partner and customer ecosystem, forming a key part of our international support model.

You’ll have the opportunity to:

Work in a truly global support function alongside our European team
Help deliver seamless, round-the-clock support to partners and customers
Work directly with Microsoft 365 and Power Platform technologies
Contribute to the continuous improvement of the Altus platform
Help shape how technical support evolves using AI and intelligent automation
Play a key role in Altus’s global growth

Ready to make an impact?

If you’re passionate about Microsoft technologies and enjoy solving real-world problems for customers, we’d love to hear from you.

At Altus, we celebrate our diverse community and are committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all. People are at the heart of all we do, and we truly believe that we are made better by bringing together people with a broad range of backgrounds, perspectives and experiences. We are committed to a respectful workplace that embraces and celebrates diversity, equity and inclusion. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds to apply!

You can find out more about us on our website – https://www.altus.pro/

Cloud Migration Engineer (AWS & Azure) | 6-Month Initial Contract

FinXL IT Professional Services is seeking a senior Cloud Migration Engineer with dual-cloud expertise to lead a significant data centre exit program. This strategic project involves migrating complex workloads from legacy physical and VMware platforms into enterprise AWS and Azure solutions.




The Opportunity

This role is a high-impact engagement focused on technical execution and institutional capability uplift. You will lead end-to-end migrations while mentoring internal engineering teams to ensure the organization becomes self-sufficient in architecting, deploying, and supporting multi-cloud services.




Duration: 6 Months Initial (Extensions highly likely)

Work Arrangement: Hybrid

Engagement: Through FinXL.com.au

Key Responsibilities

Multi-Cloud Migrations: Lead discovery, dependency mapping, and workload assessments to execute migrations to both AWS and Azure.




Migration Strategy: Define and implement rehost (lift-and-shift), replatform, refactor, or rebuild strategies based on workload suitability.




Database Modernisation: Execute end-to-end migrations of enterprise databases to native services such as Amazon RDS/Aurora and Azure SQL Database/Managed Instance.




Infrastructure Engineering: Design and support cloud infrastructure including VPCs, Virtual Networks, EC2/VMs, IAM, and security monitoring across both platforms.




Automation & IaC: Design and implement Infrastructure-as-Code frameworks using Terraform, Bicep, or CloudFormation to automate provisioning through CI/CD pipelines.




Governance & Security: Implement secure landing zones, resource tagging, cost management, and network segmentation aligned with enterprise standards.




Capability Uplift: Lead structured knowledge transfer through workshops, code reviews, and the development of repeatable operational runbooks.




Technical Requirements

Essential Expertise:

Public Cloud Mastery: Hands-on expertise in both Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Microsoft Azure infrastructure and PaaS offerings.




Migration Tooling: Proven experience with migration tools such as AWS DMS and Azure Database Migration Service.




Database Engineering: Strong understanding of database lifecycle operations (patching, upgrades, HA/DR) and performance tuning.




Automation Skills: Proficiency in Terraform and automation scripting using Python, Bash, or PowerShell.




Operational Excellence: Experience in incident response, root cause analysis, and documenting complex architecture patterns.




Desirable Skills:

Containerisation: Experience with cloud-native application patterns.




Compliance: Background in implementing security controls and encryption for regulated enterprise environments.

WHAT'S SO AMAZING ABOUT THIS OPPORTUNITY

As a Systems Performance Engineer, based at a significant client site in Tarneit, Melbourne, you'll be at the heart of optimising some of the most advanced automated materials handling systems in the industry. This role blends control systems expertise, data analytics, and customer engagement, giving you the opportunity to turn complex operational data into meaningful performance improvements.

You will work hands‑on with Warehouse Control System (WCS) and PLC data to monitor system health, diagnose faults, and measure performance against functional specifications and contractual KPIs. Developing insightful dashboards and analytics, prepare clear performance reports, and collaborate directly with customer operations and maintenance teams to deliver practical recommendations that improve system reliability, throughput, and operational efficiency.

This is a highly visible role where your insights directly influence system performance, customer satisfaction, and long‑term value creation. You'll continuously identify optimisation opportunities, develop technical solutions, and help customers get the best possible performance from their automation investments.

Hours of Work: Monday to Friday

WHAT YOU WILL BE WORKING ON

Provide second level technical support
Develop and maintain performance dashboards and analytics
Monitor, analyse, report on machine faults, trends, root causes, and operational impact
Analyse WCS and PLC data to identify performance risks and optimisation opportunities
Assess system performance against functional specifications, KPIs, and contractual service levels
Align system performance with logistics operations, SKU profiles, and production flows
Support and troubleshoot industrial and IT network interfaces between equipment, WCS, customer systems
Perform regular system diagnostics and deliver clear, professional performance reports
Provide technical guidance and performance insights to operations and maintenance teams
Prepare test plans, manuals, and technical documentation
Build strong customer relationships, translating operational challenges into actionable insights
Deliver training to customer and Dematic personnel
Resolve or escalate system issues, working closely with the Customer Incident Management team
Report on equipment not performing to design specifications
Contribute to revenue opportunities and support sales activities
Ensure compliance with OHS&E standards

SETTING YOU UP FOR SUCCESS

Degree qualified: Systems Engineering or Computer / Electrical / Mechatronics or related disciplines
2+ Years Systems Engineering experience
Knowledge of Material Handling, order fulfillment and warehouse automation
Experience in design and delivery of complex material handling systems
Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage confidently in customer facing environments
High attention to detail with a structured, analytical approach
Ability to work independently and without close supervision
Willingness to participate in after-hours support when required
Valid Driver's license
Working Rights: Citizen or PR

WHY BRING YOUR CAREER TO DEMATIC

Dematic Pty Ltd, is a world leader within the integrated logistics and material handling industry. We provide intelligent logistics solutions for manufacturing and distribution, as well as comprehensive automation and software technology solutions, tailored to our clients' needs

Our innovative portfolio meets the demands of an ever-changing supply chain landscape and is designed to deliver a clear competitive edge. As a company we take pride in offering our employees a safe and rewarding work environment in which to develop their careers

This is an exciting time to join Dematic. We are proud to design, build, implement, and support automated system solutions for warehouses, distribution centres, and production facilities. We live on the cutting edge of technology and industry and are a global leader with 11,000 employees worldwide and over 700 employees in Australia and New Zealand

And some other great things to know about us……

Employee engagement is high at 80% in our recent employee survey
Average tenure is 8 years, with 12% internal promotions and transfers annually because we actively invest in developing our people.
A staggering 30% of all new hires are referrals, as our people know it's a great place to work and recommend it to others

HOW TO APPLY

If you would like to apply for the above position, please click on the 'Apply for this job' button and submit your resume. For more information visit: www.dematic.com

About the team
You'll be joining the Data Engineering team on an exciting mission to build a top-tier data platform that powers everything we do. We manage data from our in-house products like Stake and Kick, as well as third-party tools and services, centralizing it and ensuring it's reliable, scalable, and ready to drive smarter decisions across the business.

We're focused on advanced data tools and services that truly make a difference. Our goal is to help teams unlock the full potential of data, empowering them to create impactful outcomes for our customers and the business. If you're someone who loves tackling big challenges and shaping the future of data, you'll fit right in!

Key Responsibilities

Architect, design, and optimise scalable pipelines, orchestrating complex workflows and enabling efficient data processing from multiple sources.
Lead the development of secure, scalable, and cost-efficient data infrastructure using AWS services and Terraform, ensuring engineering best practices in infrastructure automation.
Collaborate with stakeholders to define and drive strategic data initiatives, including data lakehouse architecture, real-time data processing, and analytics enablement.
Own and enhance observability practices, implementing robust monitoring, alerting, and logging systems to minimise downtime and optimise performance.
Develop and execute automated data governance processes to ensure compliance with data lineage, classification, access control, GDPR, and other regulatory standards. This includes masking, encryption, and policy enforcement to protect sensitive data, especially in regulated markets.
Architect and optimise data pipelines for Change Data Capture (CDC), reconciliation workflows, and reliability enhancements.
Drive improvements in engineering best practices, including code quality, system design, documentation, and operational efficiency, to foster team efficiency and high-quality outcomes with measurable business value.
Identify opportunities and implement innovative solutions to enhance data platform capabilities, proactively solving performance bottlenecks and improving data reliability.
Perform advanced code reviews, mentor engineers and foster a collaborative, high-performance and collaborative engineering culture.

Qualifications

A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field or equivalent practical experience.
8+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role, with demonstrated expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT design, database management, and real-time data pipelines.
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Python, or PySpark, with deep experience in building data pipelines leveraging AWS services such as Glue, Redshift, Kinesis, Lambda, S3, and DMS.
Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Apache Airflow), version control systems (e.g., GitHub), and big data technologies such as Spark or Hadoop.
Proven expertise in data governance, security, and compliance standards, particularly in regulated environments, including managing lineage, classification, access control, and PII.
Experience designing and implementing modern cloud-based data platforms, preferably on AWS, using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform.
Lead R&D efforts, exploring emerging technologies and evaluating their applications for system enhancement.
Knowledge of machine learning concepts and their integration with data platforms.
Strong problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills for engaging with cross-functional teams and addressing complex technical challenges.
Experience in technical leadership skills, including mentoring teams, providing strategic guidance, and shaping organisational strategies.
Some of the perks of joining us:
Championing Engineering Excellence to influence data driven impact across global scale software products.
Work alongside the top 5% of engineering talent in Australia using a vast AWS cloud native and big data technology stack.
Exposure to building global, large-scale volume data pipelines, data warehouses, and datalakes which are consuming requests at thousands per second frequency.

Weekly team lunches and happy hour in the office from 4pm on Fridays.
Enjoy a bustling office with the option for up to 2 days work from home per week.
Fun office environment with F1 simulators, table tennis and all your favourite gaming consoles.

About the role

Zezamii is building Zezamii Vision, an AI-powered video analytics platform for real-time event detection on live camera feeds. The business is now productising the solution into a scalable platform that combines live inference, event management, client administration, and reusable machine learning pipelines. This role leads the ML side of the product across the full lifecycle, from model fine-tuning and evaluation through to deployment support and reusable training pipelines.

Initial focus

Fine-tune and improve production computer vision models for live event detection.

Increase accuracy across edge cases, difficult scenes, and extreme scenarios.

Help create a reusable Azure ML training architecture that can support future use cases and industries.

Key responsibilities

Fine-tune and improve computer vision models for real-time event detection.

Build and maintain reusable training and retraining workflows in Azure ML.

Design robust evaluation processes, metrics, and error analysis pipelines.

Work with labelled datasets, annotation feedback loops, and data quality improvement.

Support deployment of models into production inference environments.

Collaborate with platform engineers building the live streaming, event tracking, and dashboard components.

Help define the reusable ML architecture for future use cases and industries.

Contribute to model monitoring, versioning, and continuous improvement practices.

Required skills and experience

Strong experience in Python for machine learning and data workflows.

Hands-on experience with computer vision model development and fine-tuning.

Minimum 2-3 years of experience in computer vision model development.

Experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow, preferably PyTorch.

Experience with object detection, activity recognition, video analytics, or event detection.

Experience with model training, validation, experiment tracking, and performance optimisation.

Experience deploying or supporting ML models in production environments.

Minimum 1-2 years of experience in production ML model deployment.

Experience with cloud ML platforms such as Azure ML, AWS SageMaker, or GCP Vertex AI.

Strong understanding of dataset quality, false positive/false negative trade-offs, and edge-case analysis.

Preferred experience

Azure ML Studio and Azure-based ML workflows.

Live video streams, CCTV, RTSP, or real-time inference systems.

Docker, containers, and cloud deployment pipelines.

Safety-critical or compliance-sensitive environments.

Designing reusable ML systems rather than one-off models.

What success looks like

Production models achieve improved production-grade accuracy.

The training pipeline becomes reusable across future Zezamii Vision use cases.

Model deployment becomes more repeatable and reliable.

ML workflows are well-documented and scalable.

Data Scientist - Analytics / ML

Melbourne / Sydney

$1100-1400 a day

12 month contract

Overview
We are working with a leading Financial Services client on a new role in their Data team. This role sits within a growing analytics function focused on supporting a direct-to-consumer business. The team is responsible for enabling better day-to-day decision-making through data, improving performance, and delivering actionable insights across the organisation.

You will work at the intersection of data science, analytics, and business strategy-partnering closely with teams such as marketing and digital to translate data into meaningful outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Design and deliver machine learning models to solve business problems, with an initial focus on customer segmentation and clustering

Explore and validate multiple data sources to identify opportunities for insight generation

Translate business challenges into analytical frameworks and data science solutions

Build, test, and deploy models using tools such as Python, SQL, and Databricks

Develop reporting and visualisations to communicate insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Partner with internal teams (e.g. marketing, digital) to guide targeting, performance optimisation, and decision-making

Contribute to the development of scalable data science capabilities and best practices

Monitor and track model performance, ensuring solutions are production-ready and continuously improved

Support migration and transformation of legacy analytics processes into modern data environments

Clearly explain methodologies, outputs, and impact of models to a range of stakeholders

What You'll Be Working On

Customer segmentation projects using clustering techniques

Exploratory data analysis to assess and validate available data

End-to-end model development: from discovery - build - production - monitoring

Enabling broader business use cases through incremental data science projects

Supporting the evolution of the organisation's analytics and machine learning capability

Skills & Experience

Essential:

Strong experience with Python & SQL

Hands-on experience building and deploying machine learning models

Ability to work across the full data science lifecycle

Experience working with large and complex datasets

Strong communication skills-ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences

Experience working in collaborative, cross-functional environments

Desirable:

Experience with Databricks or similar cloud-based data platforms

Familiarity with BI/reporting tools and data visualisation

Experience in customer analytics, marketing analytics, or digital analytics

Understanding of clustering, segmentation, and other unsupervised learning techniques

Exposure to agile delivery environments

Ad Text:
Position Snapshot

Location: Smithtown, NSW
Duration: Full time, permanent
3+ years’ experience relevant to this role

Position Summary

Joining Nestlé means you are joining the largest Food and Beverage Company in the world. At our very core, we are a human environment – passionate people driven by the purpose of enhancing the quality of life and contributing to a healthier future.

Fantastic Opportunity for an Electrical & Automation Engineer to join our MILO Smithtown Factory. This role will provide support on Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), Electricity and Automation (E&A) professional and technical in the Factory for operational activities and during participation in CAPEX projects.

A day in the life…

Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you will be part of a collaborative team passionate about improving factory operations. Key responsibilities include but are not limited to: 

Manage the factory electrical infrastructure from design through to installation, maintenance, operation, improvement and upgrade as part of a site electrical strategy.

Participates in Capital Investments projects in the factory ensuring that the technical solution follows corporate and market standards.

Responsible for preparation of the user and technical specifications.

Ensure Process & Equipment safety for people, products and environment and that one exhibits an attitude of absolute intolerance for unsafe situations.

Ensure that the Change Management is in place to appropriately manage modifications in the MES-E&A solutions.

Play a key role in the continuous improvement of our factories through mastering the process and product characteristics.

Supervise and coordinate Automation and MES maintenance and project work by electricians and contractors.

Co-ordinate with Production, Quality Management and Engineering to resolve Automation and MES issues.

Develop a deep insight of the processes, and continuously strive for the improvement of quality, safety or efficiency while protecting the technological know-how and competitive advantage.

Participates in the implementation of engineering tools at site level (i.e. NSAT, NEST, AMM, etc.).

Ensure that all MES-E&A installations comply with market and corporate safety, health, environment, product safety and product quality standards.

Ensures that the key MES-E&A documentation is up to date and in place (i.e. SLD, URS, FDS, Calibration procedures, etc.). Participates in the M&I activities in the factory.

What will make you successful

Joining a collaborative team of specialists, you will be a strong leader with a drive to deliver quality outcomes.

Minimum Bachelor degree in Electrical and Automation/ Mechatronics.

Minimum 5 years Automation and/or MES engineer with Electrical experience in Food and Beverages, FMCG, Pharmaceutical or similar.

Experience in Personnel Supervision of technical teams, contractors or other 3rd parties.

This is a hands-on role where you’ll combine business analysis, AI prompt engineering, and automation development to deliver meaningful, scalable outcomes.

Identify and design AI automation opportunities across the business

The Senior Proposal Engineer develops technical and commercial proposals for the assessment, design, implementation, and management of Solutions and Services offerings tailored to customer needs.




You will support Rockwell Automation Lifecycle Services' business and financial goals by delivering compliant, high-quality proposals. As a subject matter expert, the Senior Proposal Engineer collaborates with Sales, Departments, distributor and teams across the region to explain the value of Rockwell Automation's portfolio.




Your Responsibilities:

Proposal Development & Execution

Lead the development of detailed, customised proposals for Industrial Automation, Drives, Networks, and cybersecurity Services.

Accomplish proposal activities including technical assessment, risk identification and mitigation, proposal generation, approvals, and order entry.

Ensure proposals meet defined margin and compliance requirements.

Deliver accurate, professional proposals within expected timeframes.




Collaboration & Communication

Work with Sales, Solution Consultants, Engineering, Field Services, Operations, Commercial & Negotiation (C&N), Finance, and Credit Management teams.

Be a primary contact for Sales and Teams during the proposal phase.

Address customer queries and provide technical clarifications.

Review and interpret commercial terms and conditions, including corporate agreements.




Solution Design & Cost Estimation

Design solutions aligned with client specifications and industry best practices.

Develop accurate cost estimates considering materials, labour, and project-related expenses.

Ensure task completion.




Technical Expertise

Demonstrated hands-on experience in Operational Technology (OT) environments, including the design, deployment, and management of industrial automation systems..

Advanced proficiency with Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs), including migration, modification, and upgrade projects across various platforms

Knowledge in the installation, maintenance, and upgrading of Low Voltage (LV) and Medium Voltage (MV) drives, ensuring optimal performance and reliability.




Risk & Quality Management

Conduct risk assessments and propose mitigation strategies.

Ensure accuracy in all technical content.

Collaborate with quality assurance to meet internal standards.




Continuous Improvement

Stay updated on trends in industrial automation and OT/IT integration.

Provide feedback for process improvements in the proposal cycle.

Lead standardisation of proposals and tools for network and cybersecurity solutions.




Success Indicators

Deliver accurate, high-quality proposals.

Proposals align with margin targets, compliance standards and internal processes.

Support business growth and strategic projects.




The Essentials - You Will Have:

Bachelor's degree in Engineering

Minimum 8 years of experience in proposal/project engineering within industrial automation.

Technical knowledge of automation technologies (PLCs, DCS, drives).·

Understanding of cost structures, pricing strategies, and margin analysis is critical.

You will be asked to use MS office tools

Command on contract terms & conditions

Collaborative mindset.·

Fluent in English (written and spoken)

adaptable communication style.·

AEMO at the Heart of Energy   

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role 

AEMO’s Operations division is at the forefront of the energy transition, managing power system through the energy transition. This role supports two key priorities in AEMO’s Corporate Plan: operating today's systems and markets and navigating the energy future. 

As a Senior Engineer in the Network Modelling and Information team you will play an important role in driving AEMO and industry modelling capability and the future global direction of Power System Modelling. You will lead the management of power system data and development of power system study base cases (RMS and EMT), AEMO’s data request function, and support the uplift of operations technology. This role also has a focus on maintaining Power System Modelling information as well as championing model integrity and contributing to the Power System Modelling capability within AEMO. 

What you will be doing  

Integrate new models into and update existing models in National Electricity Market (NEM) wide-area RMS and EMT base cases. Perform simulation on the updated base cases to ascertain the model quality.

Develop and tune seasonal snapshots, RMS and EMT bases cases for the NEM with large quantities of inverter-based generation and customer energy resources.

Develop and maintain power system models for small signal stability analysis.

Prepare clear, concise, and high quality written and verbal communications including reports, stakeholder presentations, information packs, briefing papers; contribute to uplifting network modelling and data request management efficiency and quality.

Assess future requirements for power system modelling, including improving simulation speed, identifying model limitations, migration to new software versions and other essential power system modelling needs. 

What you will bring to the role   

Tertiary qualifications in Electrical Engineering with a discipline relevant to power systems.

Demonstrated experience in electricity transmission network analysis and the interpretation of analysis performed by others; and experience in electrical power system dynamics, stability concepts, and assessment techniques.

Understanding and knowledge of the physical operation of the electricity transmission systems, network constraints, and the overarching markets.

Demonstrated experience in power systems analysis simulation applications such as PSCAD, PSS/E. Experience in PowerFactory, Mudpack and SSAT as well as other analytical software such as MATLAB is desirable.

Proficiency in automation of processes and programs with an object-oriented programming language (Python, Fortran etc.) is desirable. 

In return some of our benefits to you 

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more. 

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.  

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).  

About Our Process  

AEMO values diversity and inclusivity in the workplace, welcoming applications from all backgrounds without regard to age, disability, gender, sexual orientation, parental status, race, religion and our First Nation peoples. We are dedicated to accommodating applicants' needs during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing talent@aemo.com.au.

The Company:

This well known digital product leader have been growing from strength to strength and leads the way in their industry with world class customer facing web and mobile products that are all data driven.

With some of the strongest and most engaging products out in the market, they have been offering robust and highly effective personalised products to their customers for many years.

With the creation of an Enterprise AI Division, AI is playing a big part in the further optimisation of products and platforms within the business.

The Role:

A passionate and innovative Senior AI Engineer is required to sit at the intersection of platform and product (agents). You will be building agents directly using the SDK while improving platform to lower the barrier to adoption for rest of the engineering teams.

Technical Experience Required:

Experience Building Agents (With Frontend Skills) - Design and ship full-stack agentic experiences: chat UIs, streaming responses, and tool-call visualisation.
Expertise building MCP Servers - Author Model Context Protocol servers, define tool schemas, and integrate them into LLM workflows.
Building Agents with User Auth - Secure agentic systems with identity (OAuth2/OIDC, JWT), scoped tool permissions, and session-aware multi-turn conversations.
Agent Observability & Evals - Instrument agents with tracing (LangSmith, OTEL), define evals, and iterate on reliability in production.
Agent-to-Agent (A2A) (nice to have) - Design multi-agent systems with task delegation, inter-agent communication, and orchestration patterns (supervisor, DAG, swarm).
Soft Skills:

Makes forward progress with ambiguous requirements and shifting model capabilities; documents decisions as they go.
Sets direction without a playbook, unblocks others, and knows when to prototype vs. productionise.
Works fluidly across traditional engineers, AI platform engineers, and product designers; drives alignment without formal authority.
Flags when something isn't working, swaps approaches quickly, shares learnings back to the team; avoids sunk-cost thinking.

About the role

Working as Team Lead – Business Performance Analytics (BI) your role will be to work with team members and stakeholder to improve regulatory processes and outcomes showcasing your skills in technical leadership, team management, solution design and architecture with a focus on producing quality outcomes.

Key responsibilities of this role are:

Act as a hands-on technical lead for the team that models and represents insights and data products in support of data-driven decision making at the NHVR.
In collaboration with the Data Engineering and Data Platform teams, be accountable for the production and maintenance of analytical data models in the Enterprise Data Platform.
Provide authoritative advice to Managers, Directors, and other senior staff across the organisation in creating and using products that support data-driven decision making.
Manage the development of project plans from rough business requirements and collaborate across multi-disciplinary teams to adhere to project roadmaps.
Write technical specifications using industry standard formats, peer review technical writing of team members, and ensure architectural diagrams and technical specifications are up to date.
Participate, collaborate, and help facilitate Agile Program Management processes that foster high performing SCRUM teams to deliver key BAU activities and milestones from the Project Roadmap and Strategic plans.

NHVR’s Data Science and Analytics Unit are currently undertaking growth and considerable transformation, and this is an exciting time to join the organisation.

About You

We are seeking a dedicated professional with relevant tertiary degree and a minimum of 7 years IT experience and at least 2 years in a technical supervisory capacity.

You will have extensive experience in Business Intelligence, reporting, analysis and data modelling coupled with experience with cloud-based data services and data warehouses (e.g., Microsoft Azure services, Databricks – ideally holding certifications in both).

Demonstrated experience in forming and leading high-performance technical teams.

Proven aptitude at evaluating and applying new technologies or methods to address future business needs.

 
Why join us?

The National Heavy Vehicle Regulator (NHVR) commenced operations in 2013 as the national entity responsible for regulating all vehicles in Australia over 4.5 tonnes gross vehicle mass. The NHVR minimises the compliance burden on the heavy vehicle transport industry, reduces duplication and inconsistences across state and territory borders, and ultimately for the heavy vehicle business with government in Australia.

We are headquartered in Brisbane and employ more than 1000 people across the ACT, New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania and Victoria.


We offer-

A competitive salary package from $148,069.84 + superannuation
Monthly RDO and paid leave over Christmas closure.
Hybrid working environment – up to 2 days from home.
Up to15.25% superannuation through a co-contribution scheme
Leave loading 17.5%
Wellbeing programs – discounted fitness through Fitness Passport, Discount health insurance, flu vaccine, financial programs including discounted banking products, finance information sessions.
Salary packaging options including novated lease.
Discounted IT products including Microsoft and free family password security plan.
Ongoing training and professional development support
Professional membership

The Opportunity

Mission Data (MD) Division within the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) is responsible for providing the foundational data capabilities to support all of ASD’s functions. MD Division does this via the provision of data platforms and services, management of ASD’s mission data, and providing complex analytic development and support, including Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning capabilities. All roles within MD require extensive knowledge of and compliance with legislative frameworks, government decision-making and Defence’s mission and policies requirements.

Teamwork, communication, and problem solving skills are essential as you apply your technical skills to a range of data engineering tasks and a constantly evolving technology ecosystem. You will work in a multi-disciplinary environment on some of ASD’s most important data capabilities that support intelligence, cyber security, and offensive cyber operations.

ASD 4 Data Engineer

Assist in the design and implementation of data flows to connect operational systems and analytics platforms, ensuring compliance with data security and governance requirements.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of components of enterprise data warehouse and data engineering solutions.
Work with stakeholders to gather and prepare medium scale data sets that meet defined business requirements.
Support the build and operation of data streaming pipelines.
Undertake initial root cause analysis on data issues and processes under guidance.
Contribute to the development and application of data engineering frameworks, standards and best practices.
Support data model development and assist in making improvements aligned with business needs.
Plan own work, maintain task schedules and provide routine progress updates to supervisors.

ASD 5 Data Engineer

Lead the design and implementation of complex data flows that integrate operational systems with analytics and business intelligence environments, ensuring robust data security and governance.
Oversee the design, development and maintenance of enterprise data warehouse and data engineering solutions, ensuring alignment with architectural direction.
Engage with diverse stakeholders to understand analytical requirements and assemble large, complex data sets that support strategic and operational decision making.
Lead the build, optimisation and maintenance of data streaming systems.
Conduct detailed root cause analysis to resolve complex data issues and identify enhancements to processes and systems.
Develop and refine data engineering frameworks, standards and best practices to improve data availability, performance and security.
Lead the creation and optimisation of data models in response to evolving business requirements.
Develop and manage work plans, coordinate activities across contributors, and report on progress, risks and outcomes.

ASD 6 Data Engineer

Provide skilled leadership in the design and implementation of complex, enterprise wide data flows and integrations, ensuring they meet strategic, security and governance requirements.
Lead and direct the architectural design, development and long term management of enterprise data warehouse and data engineering platforms.
Shape strategic engagement with senior stakeholders to identify emerging data needs and design solutions that support organisational priorities.
Oversee the design and delivery of scalable, enterprise grade data streaming capabilities and advanced data processing pipelines.
Lead complex, Agency wide root cause analysis activities to resolve systemic data issues and drive continuous improvement.
Set the strategic direction for data engineering frameworks, standards and governance processes, ensuring consistent adoption across teams.
Provide authoritative guidance on data model design and evolution for enterprise systems.
Lead program level planning, manage priorities across teams or projects, and provide high level reporting on outcomes, risks and alignment to strategic objectives.

Further information can be found at: I’m changing my career | Australian Signals Directorate (asd.gov.au).

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist – Applied AI & Data Engineering
Ad Text:
This is: the job
We’re looking for a hands-on builder, someone who thinks like a data engineer or software developer first, and a data scientist second.

This is a 12-month initial contract based in Melbourne, with strong potential to extend, working within a large enterprise environment delivering real AI capability at scale.

This role sits at the intersection of AI, data engineering and software development, focused on building production-grade AI systems from the ground up. You won’t be handing off notebooks, you’ll be owning problems end-to-end, from messy raw data through to deployed, scalable solutions.

The work centres around LLMs, NLP, recommendation systems and modern AI pipelines, all within an enterprise Azure environment. If you enjoy building robust systems, writing clean code, and shipping solutions that actually get used, this is for you.

This is: what you’ll do
Design and build end-to-end AI and data pipelines, from ingestion through to production deployment
Develop embedding-based search and recommendation systems, including vector databases and similarity search
Build and deploy NLP and LLM pipelines to extract insights from unstructured data
Write production-quality, scalable code, not one-off scripts or notebooks

Work closely with stakeholders to translate technical solutions into real business impact

Strong proficiency in the modern AI and ML stack including PyTorch or TensorFlow, HuggingFace Transformers, scikit-learn, pandas and related tools
Proven experience building data pipelines and production ML systems, not just models
Experience with embeddings, vector search, recommendation systems or RAG architectures
Strong experience with NLP and or LLM-based solutions
Hands-on experience with data engineering tools such as Spark, Databricks, Airflow or similar
Experience working with Azure cloud services including Azure ML, Azure OpenAI, Synapse or similar
Solid understanding of software engineering practices including Git, CI/CD, testing and code reviews
Ability to operate independently and take ownership of complex technical problems
This is: the perks
Build real, production AI systems, not just proofs of concept
Work with modern AI technologies including LLMs, embeddings and vector search
High-impact role influencing how AI is delivered at enterprise scale
Join a team that values engineering quality, ownership and delivery

Apply with purpose. Be part of a placement that gives back. For every role we fill, we donate to a charity of the client’s choice. We’ve surpassed $1 million donated and we’re just getting started.

Job Title: AI Automation Engineer | Full Stack Developer
Ad Text:
Welcome to Lakestone.ai, Perth's fastest growing AI agency.

We build complete AI-driven production systems for corporate clients, and we're very good at it.

We're a small, dedicated, passionate, high-output team but first and foremost we're about developing a fun, engaging culture of learning and sharing.

 

What we offer:

Competitive salary + performance-based incentives/bonuses

Genuine flexibility - work where and when you're most productive

Variety - Interesting, varied work across multiple industries, projects, and tech stacks

A brilliant team - you'll learn from — and who'll learn from you!

 

The work:

No two weeks look the same. Our clients trust us with their most complex problems and data. You'll work across multiple projects, multiple stacks and multiple industries.

Recent builds include:

Custom CRM dashboards with real-time analytics

RAG pipelines with pgvector and semantic search

Speech-to-text processing systems

AI-powered chatbots and conversational agents

Internal tooling that replaces entire manual workflows

 

How we operate:

Output is the only metric. No standups. No timesheets. No micromanagement.

You get flexibility, autonomy and performance bonuses. We care about what you ship, not when you're online.

Some days you'll be at a client's office, sitting with their team, understanding the problem firsthand and shipping solutions in real time.

Other days you'll be working from home, heads down on a build. You manage your own schedule — the balance between client-facing and deep work is yours to own.

Note: This means two things: you need to be genuinely comfortable engaging with non-technical clients and end-users face-to-face, and you need to be excellent at managing yourself.

 

Location:

Perth-based only. You will need to be at client sites regularly. Candidates must have full working rights in Australia to be considered.

 

The Requirements (there are only two):

(1) You must know Claude Code inside and out.

You need to demonstrate deep, working knowledge of:

Customisation - Skills, hooks, MCP servers, rules, and custom agents

Multi-worktree workflows — planning, execution, and review running simultaneously

Memory systems — project memory, session context management, episodic/semantic/hybrid

Model tiering — orchestrating Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku across parallel sub-agents

Third-party integrations — MCPs, skills, and agent ecosystems




(2) Show us what you've built.

No lengthy CVs. No cover letters. No "proficient in AI tools." No X+ years of previous software experience or university degrees required.

We want to see a repo, a demo, or a screen recording. Show us something that proves you build real things with AI-assisted development. We'll be looking at your architecture, security practices, code structure, and how you manage complexity — this will be our reference check.




The Tech Stack:

You should be very comfortable across most of these:

- TypeScript, React, Vite, Tailwind CSS

- Hono, Express, Node.js, Bun

- Supabase (Postgres), MongoDB

- Vercel, Cloudflare (Workers, Pages), Railway, Fly

- Docker (local + production)

- GitHub Actions, CI/CD pipelines

- Microsoft 365 tenant administration

 

How to Apply:

Skip the SEEK Apply button. Send an email directly to join@lakestone.ai with your contact details and links to your best work—repos, demos, screen recordings, or architecture diagrams. This is what we'll evaluate.
